zh-tw.title: 股票投資人語錄
zh-tw.description: 台股創新高、帳面創新低——獻給每個被叫做韭菜、卻還在自稱長期投資者的散戶。

en.title: Stock Investor Quotes
en.description: All-time highs on the index, all-time lows in my portfolio. For every retail trader who calls a loss a 'long-term investment'.

items.sourceNote: 5/23 台股 42,267 史上新高 + 1,172 家齊揚；改寫原創 K 型對比 register

zh-tw.content:
新聞：「台股今日收盤再創歷史新高，逾千家個股齊揚。」
我：「逾千家齊揚，沒有一家是我的。我的帳戶，獨自走自己的路。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 丟在投資群組，給每個邊看新聞邊重新整理庫存的同好
variations: 大盤紅通通的日子，最適合用來檢討個人選股能力。
媒體說全民賺錢，我看了一下對帳單，覺得自己不算全民。

en.content:
Headline: 'Market hits a fresh all-time high, over a thousand names in the green.'
Me: 'Over a thousand in green. Not a single one is mine. My account walks its own path.'
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat with every friend refreshing their brokerage app on a green day
variations: On the days the index is glowing red, take a moment to evaluate your stock-picking abilities.
They say everyone is winning. I checked my statement and decided I'm not part of everyone.

items.sourceNote: 「韭菜」散戶 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
別人問我是哪一派的投資人。
我說我自成一派：被割派。
韭菜的自我修養，第一條就是——下次還是會買在最高點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放在 IG 限動或券商 APP 截圖旁邊，配上一個下跌的紅K
variations: 我不是不會看圖，我只是看完之後永遠選錯邊。
我跟主力的關係很單純，他出貨，我接貨。

en.content:
People ask what kind of investor I am.
I tell them I'm in a class of my own: the exit liquidity class.
Rule one of being retail — next time, I'll still buy the top.
en.editorNote: Pair with a screenshot of a red candle in your story, perfect captioning for any down day
variations: It's not that I can't read charts. I just always pick the wrong side after I do.
My relationship with the whales is simple. They sell. I buy.

items.sourceNote: PTT 股板「套牢=長期投資」 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友：「你這支抱多久了？」
我：「三年。」
朋友：「哇你真的是價值投資者耶。」
我：「不是，是我捨不得停損，怕一賣它就漲。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每一個把『被套牢』講成『長期持有』的同溫層
variations: 長期投資的真實定義：賠太多不敢賣。
我不是不賣，我是在等它回到我當初買的價格，然後跟它說再見。

en.content:
Friend: 'How long have you been holding that one?'
Me: 'Three years.'
Friend: 'Wow, true value investor.'
Me: 'No, I just can't bring myself to take the loss in case it bounces the second I sell.'
en.editorNote: Dedicated to everyone who calls 'underwater for years' a 'long-term position'
variations: Long-term investing, real definition: down too much to admit it.
I'm not holding. I'm waiting for it to return to my entry so I can say goodbye in peace.

items.sourceNote: 追高仔 register / 「家裡死人嗎」 主力出貨自嘲；改寫原創 (避開原始 vulgar 用語)

zh-tw.content:
我點開那檔股票的當下，它正在開派對。
我刷卡進場的那一秒，主力默默把燈關了，桌上的酒收一收，跟我說：「啊歹勢，今天到這裡。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在收盤後對著沖天炮變成跌停板的對帳單前發呆時用
variations: 我進場的時機很神奇，神奇到主力都會回頭看我一眼。
我的買進均價，就是主力的退場提示音。

en.content:
I opened the chart and the party was in full swing.
The moment my order filled, the whales quietly turned off the lights, cleared the table, and said: 'Sorry pal, that's a wrap for tonight.'
en.editorNote: For staring at a candle that went from rocket to red after the close
variations: My entry timing is so magical, even institutional traders turn around to look.
My average cost is basically the exit signal for everyone smarter than me.

items.sourceNote: 全民投資年 + K型經濟 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
媒體說：「2026 是全民投資年。」
我：「對，全民都在投資，只是我投的是補稅，不是基金。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 5/31 報稅末日後配電費通知一起看更有 K 型味
variations: 全民投資年的真實意思是：你不投資，通膨就會替你投資。
我有一個多元化投資組合：補稅、電費、房租，三大產業齊揚。

en.content:
Media: '2026 is the year of the retail investor.'
Me: 'True. We're all investing — I'm just investing in back taxes, not index funds.'
en.editorNote: Pair with your tax deadline reminder for full K-shaped recession energy
variations: The real meaning of 'everyone's investing now' is: if you don't, inflation will do it for you.
I have a diversified portfolio — taxes, rent, utilities. Three strong sectors, all up YoY.

items.sourceNote: 技術分析自欺 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我學了均線、學了 KD、學了 MACD，學了波浪理論。
畫了三條趨勢線、兩個支撐位、一個頸線。
結論：跌。
原因：我有買。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個花了一個月學技術分析、結果還是賠錢的朋友
variations: 技術指標準不準我不知道，但我準。我準準的買在最高點。
圖表上的每一條線都會告訴我不同的故事，但我永遠選最樂觀的那一條來信。

en.content:
I learned moving averages. I learned RSI. I learned MACD. I learned Elliott Wave.
I drew three trendlines, two support levels, one neckline.
Conclusion: it will go down.
Reason: I bought.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who took a TA course and is still down 30%
variations: I don't know if technical indicators are accurate. But I am. Accurately at the top, every time.
Every line on the chart tells me a different story. I always believe the most optimistic one.

items.sourceNote: 親友報明牌 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
親戚過年塞給我一支明牌，說：「這個你買，下半年保證翻倍。」
半年後，他翻倍了：本來欠我一萬，現在欠我兩萬。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合丟給每個過年被親戚『好心報牌』整慘的同事
variations: 親戚的明牌跟天氣預報一樣，講得很有信心，每次都打臉。
唯一不會虧的方式，是把親戚的明牌反著做。但這樣親戚會生氣。

en.content:
Uncle slipped me a hot tip at Thanksgiving: 'Buy this, guaranteed double by year-end.'
Six months later, it did double — the amount he owes me.
en.editorNote: Forward to anyone who got 'helpful stock advice' from family at the last holiday dinner
variations: Family stock tips are like weather forecasts: delivered with full confidence, wrong every single time.
The only foolproof strategy is to fade your uncle. But then he stops talking to you.

items.sourceNote: 停損 vs 攤平 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
投資老師說：「不要逆勢攤平。」
我說：「老師我懂了。」
然後跌了 10%，我加碼。
再跌 10%，我又加碼。
現在均價很漂亮，跟我的存款一樣，幾乎見底。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在『再加碼一次就好』的當下提醒自己冷靜
variations: 攤平的盡頭不是解套，是攤到平躺在地上。
我的均價是用無數次『這次一定是最低點』堆出來的。

en.content:
Investing book: 'Never average down on a losing trade.'
Me: 'Got it.'
Then it drops 10%, I buy more.
Drops another 10%, I buy more.
Now my average cost is beautiful — and so is the bottom of my bank account.
en.editorNote: Tape this to your monitor before clicking 'buy more' on a position that's already underwater
variations: The end of averaging down isn't breaking even. It's lying flat on the ground.
My cost basis is built on a hundred separate moments of 'this has to be the bottom.'

items.sourceNote: 看盤的姿勢 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
9 點到 13 點半，老闆以為我在打報表。
其實我在打開券商 APP。
13 點 30 分一收盤，我才開始打報表，速度跟剛剛刷新庫存一樣快。
zh-tw.editorNote: 懂的同事自然會懂，分享前先確認老闆沒在你後面
variations: 我的工作效率，跟大盤呈現負相關。
開盤時我多工，收盤時我專心，這就是平衡。

en.content:
9 to 4, my boss thinks I'm crushing the quarterly report.
Really I'm refreshing the brokerage app.
The second the closing bell rings, I crush that report — at the same speed I was checking quotes.
en.editorNote: Coworkers will instantly relate. Check that your boss isn't behind you before sharing
variations: My work productivity is inversely correlated with the market being open.
While the market is open, I multitask. After close, I focus. That's called balance.

items.sourceNote: 賣飛 + FOMO register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我賣掉那支股票的下一秒，它開始漲。
我重新買回去的下一秒，它開始跌。
券商客服說：「先生，您的帳戶可能跟主力的方向感天生互斥。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個剛經歷『賣飛 → 追回 → 套牢』完整三部曲的人
variations: 想知道一支股票會不會漲，最快的方法就是把它賣掉。
我有一個超能力：只要我進場，趨勢就會立刻翻轉。

en.content:
The second I sold it, it ripped.
The second I bought it back, it tanked.
My broker's support line: 'Sir, your account appears to have a natural inverse correlation with the institutional flow.'
en.editorNote: For anyone who just lived through the full sold-the-bottom-bought-the-top arc
variations: Quickest way to find out if a stock will pump? Sell it.
I have a superpower: the trend reverses the instant I enter.

items.sourceNote: 牛市熊市 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
別人的市場：牛市、熊市。
我的市場：牛市、熊市，跟我自己一個人在淒風苦雨中的市。
大家都賺錢的時候，我學到了一個新的市場名字——「個人空頭市場」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在大盤漲、自己賠的日子，找個沒人的地方默默轉發
variations: 歷史上的多頭市場、空頭市場我都看過，但只有我擁有同一週兼具兩者的能力。
別人的 K 線跟我的 K 線，根本是兩個世界的故事。

en.content:
Other people's markets: bull market, bear market.
My market: bull market, bear market, and 'me standing alone in a personal blizzard.'
When everyone is winning, I learned a new market type — the 'personal recession.'
en.editorNote: For the days the index is green and your portfolio is bleeding — silent repost only
variations: I've lived through every kind of market. I'm also the only person who runs all of them in the same week.
Other people's candles and my candles tell completely different stories.

items.sourceNote: 聯發科 漲 899 點 carry + 月薪沒漲對比；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
公司今年發股利，每股配 5 元。
我月薪今年漲了 200 元。
結論：當公司股東比當公司員工划算。
但問題是，我兩個都是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 丟在公司茶水間 LINE 群，配著薪資調整通知一起看
variations: 股東會說的數字很漂亮，員工會說的數字……沒有員工會。
我看到自己公司配的股利，第一個反應是：早知道我多買一張就不用上班了。

en.content:
Company announces this year's dividend: five bucks per share.
My annual raise: six dollars and fifty cents.
Conclusion: being a shareholder pays better than being an employee.
Problem is, I'm both.
en.editorNote: For the office break room group chat, right next to the annual comp review email
variations: The numbers at the shareholder meeting look beautiful. The numbers at the employee meeting — there is no employee meeting.
I saw my own company's dividend payout and immediately thought: I should have bought more shares and quit.

items.sourceNote: 退休夢 / FIRE register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我原本的計畫：30 歲財務自由，提早退休去環遊世界。
修正版計畫：60 歲財務自由，提早退休去環遊台灣。
最新版計畫：65 歲財務沒自由，繼續上班順便環遊辦公室。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在打開試算表算退休時，提醒自己保持幽默感
variations: 我這輩子最大的投資失敗，是相信『年輕時辛苦一下，老了就輕鬆』。
FIRE 的 F 我做到了——Financial。R E 還在努力。

en.content:
Original plan: financial independence by 30, early retirement, travel the world.
Revised plan: financial independence by 60, early retirement, travel the country.
Current plan: no financial independence by 65, keep working, travel from desk to break room.
en.editorNote: Read whenever you open the retirement calculator. Keep your sense of humor intact
variations: My biggest investing mistake was believing 'work hard now, relax later.'
I achieved the F in FIRE — Financial. Still working on the R and the E.

items.sourceNote: ETF 月配息 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
他們說，存月配息 ETF，就像領第二份薪水。
我存了兩年。
第一份薪水繳房租，第二份薪水繳第一份薪水的稅。
結論：我有兩份薪水，但是我自己沒看過任何一份。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個 ETF 存股族，配著證券交易稅單一起服用
variations: 月配息 ETF 的『月配息』是真的，『讓你變有錢』是夢想。
我把股利再投入，存了一個寂寞。

en.content:
They said: stack monthly-pay ETFs and it's like a second paycheck.
I stacked for two years.
The first paycheck pays rent. The second paycheck pays taxes on the first paycheck.
Net result: I have two paychecks, and I've personally seen neither.
en.editorNote: Dedicated to dividend-ETF enthusiasts. Take with a side of capital gains tax statements
variations: The 'monthly income' part of monthly income ETFs is real. The 'gets you rich' part is aspirational.
I reinvested all the dividends and accumulated a beautiful pile of nothing.

items.sourceNote: 畢業 / 砍倉 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
股板上有一種文章叫做『畢業文』。
意思是賠光出場，正式離開股市。
我已經畢業三次了。
但每次發完畢業文，三個月後就會再回來開學。
我這輩子最持續的學習行為，居然是被市場教育。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個發過畢業文又默默回鍋的同學
variations: 畢業文的真實意思是：先休息一下，等我存到下一筆錢再來。
我在這個市場拿到的學歷比正規教育還高，但學費也是。

en.content:
There's a kind of post on trading forums called 'the graduation post'.
It means you blew up your account and are leaving for good.
I've graduated three times.
Three months after every graduation, I enroll again.
The most consistent learning of my life turned out to be getting schooled by the market.
en.editorNote: For everyone who has posted 'I'm done' and quietly come back
variations: What 'I'm done with the market' really means: I need to save up for next semester's tuition.
I've earned more degrees from this market than from college. The tuition bills agree.

items.sourceNote: 「逢低買進」無限迴圈 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
他們說：逢低買進。
於是它低了，我買了。
然後它又更低了，我發現原來剛剛那個不是低，是半山腰。
現在我終於懂了：所謂的低，是一個會一直往下修的浮動目標。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在每次點下『買進』前，提醒自己那個低點可能還有地下室
variations: 逢低買進的最大難題，是你永遠不知道低的下面還有更低。
我每一次都買在低點，只是市場後來重新定義了什麼叫低。

en.content:
They said: buy the dip.
So it dipped, and I bought.
Then it dipped harder, and I realized that first dip was actually the rooftop.
Now I finally get it: 'the bottom' is a floating target that keeps revising itself lower.
en.editorNote: Read this before clicking 'buy' — that dip may have a basement
variations: The hardest part of buying the dip is that the dip always has a sub-basement.
I buy every bottom perfectly. The market just keeps redefining what a bottom is.

items.sourceNote: 瞞著另一半偷買股票 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我跟另一半的財務很透明，除了那個我偷偷開的證券戶。
那檔股票漲的時候，我每天想找機會講。
那檔股票跌的時候，我每天祈禱她永遠不要問。
結果它跌了，現在這個秘密戶頭，變成我們家最大的未爆彈。
zh-tw.editorNote: 懂的人會心一笑，分享前先確認手機沒被借走
variations: 夫妻之間最深的信任，就是對方不知道你那個賠錢的證券戶。
我帳戶賺錢叫做投資，賠錢叫做隱私。

en.content:
My partner and I are totally transparent about money — except for the brokerage account I opened in secret.
When the stock was up, I looked for a chance to mention it every day.
When it was down, I prayed daily that she'd never ask.
It went down. That secret account is now the biggest landmine in our household.
en.editorNote: Relatable smile guaranteed. Make sure nobody borrowed your phone before sharing
variations: The deepest trust in a marriage is your partner not knowing about your underwater brokerage account.
When my account is up, it's called investing. When it's down, it's called privacy.

items.sourceNote: AI 晶片 FOMO 追高 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
所有財經頻道都在喊：AI 是未來，晶片股錯過就再也回不來了。
我怕錯過，於是在最高點全押。
結果未來來了，AI 也是未來，只是我的本金留在過去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被『再不買就來不及』推坑、結果套在山頂的朋友
variations: AI 取代了很多工作，目前最成功的一項，是取代我的本金。
我相信 AI 會改變世界，它確實改變了，從我的帳戶開始。

en.content:
Every finance channel was screaming: AI is the future, miss the chip stocks and you'll never get another shot.
Terrified of missing out, I went all in at the top.
The future arrived. AI really is the future. My capital just stayed in the past.
en.editorNote: For anyone FOMO'd into 'buy now or be priced out forever' and left holding the peak
variations: AI has replaced a lot of jobs. Its most successful replacement so far is my principal.
I believe AI will change the world. It already changed mine, starting with my account balance.

items.sourceNote: 當沖鬼才 / 手續費貢獻 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我當沖一整天，買進賣出三十幾趟，盯著螢幕盯到眼睛快脫窗。
收盤結算：本金沒什麼變。
券商結算：今天他們又多了一個孝順客戶。
原來我不是在投資，我是在做公益。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在當沖一整天、結果只繳了一堆手續費給券商的傍晚
variations: 當沖的真諦：用一整天的專注，把獲利精準轉帳給券商。
我不是賺不到錢，我是把賺到的錢都拿去養券商了。

en.content:
I day-traded all day, in and out thirty-odd times, eyes glued to the screen till they nearly fell out.
My account at close: basically unchanged.
My broker at close: another devoted customer in the books.
Turns out I wasn't investing. I was running a charity.
en.editorNote: For the evening after a full day of scalping that only fed the broker's fee column
variations: The true meaning of day trading: spend all day laser-focused on wiring your profits to your broker.
It's not that I can't make money. I just donate all of it to commissions.

items.sourceNote: 理專 / 投資建議 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
理專跟我說：「這檔很穩，閉著眼睛買就對了。」
我閉著眼睛買了。
等我睜開眼睛，本金也閉上了眼睛。
後來我才發現，他叫我閉眼睛，是因為接下來的畫面真的不適合直視。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被『這檔很穩』四個字推坑的同好
variations: 理專推薦的『穩健標的』，穩健地把我的錢帶走了。
他賺的是手續費，穩的是他的業績，賠的是我。

en.content:
My advisor said: 'This one's rock solid, buy it with your eyes closed.'
So I bought it with my eyes closed.
When I opened them, my principal had closed its eyes too — permanently.
Turns out he told me to shut my eyes because what came next really wasn't safe to watch.
en.editorNote: For everyone lured in by the four magic words 'this one is safe'
variations: The 'stable, conservative pick' my advisor recommended stably and conservatively walked off with my money.
He earns the fees, his numbers stay solid, and I'm the one who takes the loss.

items.sourceNote: 5月台股暴漲1560點 / 散戶砍在阿呆谷 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
前一天我終於鼓起勇氣停損，含淚把它砍在最低點。
隔天大盤暴漲，那檔股票直接拉一根長紅，漲幅是我這輩子看過最漂亮的。
我才明白，原來『阿呆谷』不是地名，是專門為我一個人開的觀景台。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個剛停損完隔天就看著它暴漲的同學，貼在群組互相取暖
variations: 我的停損鍵，是這支股票最可靠的買進訊號。
我砍倉的那一刻，主力在另一頭按下了反攻的按鈕。

en.content:
The day before, I finally found the courage to cut my loss and sold it at the very bottom, in tears.
The next day the whole market exploded, and that stock ripped a massive green candle — the prettiest one I've ever seen.
That's when I understood: 'Dummy Valley' isn't a place, it's a scenic overlook built just for me.
en.editorNote: For everyone who capitulated yesterday and watched it moon today — share in the group chat for mutual comfort
variations: My stop-loss button is this stock's most reliable buy signal.
The instant I cut my position, the whales hit the launch button on the other end.

items.sourceNote: 投資 app 推播 / 血壓 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
券商 APP 很貼心，跌的時候會推播提醒我，漲的時候也會推播提醒我。
所以現在我的手機，一天到晚都在提醒我心跳。
別人靠運動量手環測心率，我靠庫存損益。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被一連串紅綠推播轟炸到血壓飆高的盤中分享
variations: 我關掉了所有 APP 的通知，只留下券商的，因為那是唯一會讓我有情緒的。
我的血壓跟大盤同步，這大概是我跟市場唯一的正相關。

en.content:
My broker app is so thoughtful — it pings me when things go down, and it pings me when things go up.
So now my phone spends all day reminding me I still have a heartbeat.
Other people track their pulse with a fitness band. I track mine with unrealized P&L.
en.editorNote: For the intraday moment when a barrage of red-and-green push alerts sends your blood pressure soaring
variations: I muted every app's notifications except the broker's, because it's the only one that still makes me feel something.
My blood pressure is perfectly synced with the index. It's probably the only positive correlation I have with the market.

items.sourceNote: 「太多 dips」減肥 / 攤平自嘲 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我的投資組合跟我的減肥計畫有一個共同點：兩個都太多次『再吃一口就好』。
股票跌了，我說『逢低再買一口』。
宵夜來了，我說『就這一口』。
結果體重跟均價，雙雙創新高。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在邊吃宵夜邊攤平、雙重崩潰的深夜發
variations: 我攤平股票的決心，跟我戒宵夜的決心一樣堅定，也一樣短命。
減肥和停損，是我這輩子最常重新開始、也最常失敗的兩件事。

en.content:
My portfolio and my diet have one thing in common: both have way too many rounds of 'just one more bite.'
The stock drops, I say 'one more nibble on the dip.'
The midnight snack appears, I say 'just this one.'
Result: my weight and my cost basis both hit all-time highs.
en.editorNote: For the late-night double meltdown of averaging down while eating a midnight snack
variations: My resolve to stop averaging down is as firm as my resolve to skip dessert — and just as short-lived.
Dieting and cutting losses are the two things I've restarted, and failed at, the most in my life.

items.sourceNote: 做完功課還是賠 / 研究越多虧越多 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我認真研究了一個月，看財報、看法說會、看產業報告，做了滿滿三頁筆記。
我朋友隨便聽到一個名字就買了。
結果他賺，我賠。
我終於懂了：在這個市場，做功課唯一的功能，是讓你輸得很有學問。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個花大把時間研究、結果還輸給亂買朋友的認真仔
variations: 我研究得越深入，賠得越有條理。
別人用直覺賺錢，我用 Excel 賠錢，而且賠得很精準。

en.content:
I researched hard for a month — read the filings, watched the earnings calls, dug through industry reports, filled three pages of notes.
My friend heard a ticker once and bought it.
He's up. I'm down.
Now I finally get it: in this market, doing your homework only guarantees you lose with great academic rigor.
en.editorNote: For the diligent type who spends hours on research and still loses to a friend who buys at random
variations: The deeper my research, the more methodically I lose.
Other people make money on instinct. I lose money in Excel — and very precisely.

items.sourceNote: 美股盤 / 半夜起床看盤 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
自從買了美股，我多了一個半夜的室友，叫做時差。
台灣半夜兩點，美股正熱鬧，我準時起床盯盤。
隔天上班同事問我怎麼黑眼圈這麼重。
我說我在跨國工作，只是公司沒發薪水，還倒扣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個為了美股犧牲睡眠、白天還要上班的夜貓投資人
variations: 我不是失眠，我是配合華爾街的作息。
白天上班是為了存錢，半夜看盤是為了把錢還回去。

en.content:
Ever since I started trading US stocks, I gained a new roommate at night called the time difference.
2 a.m. Taiwan time, Wall Street is buzzing, and I wake up right on schedule to watch the screen.
Next day at work, a coworker asks why my eye bags are so heavy.
I tell them I work an international job — the company just doesn't pay me, it deducts.
en.editorNote: For the night-owl investor sacrificing sleep for the US session and still showing up to the day job
variations: I'm not an insomniac. I'm just on Wall Street hours.
I work my day job to save money, and watch the overnight session to give it all back.

items.sourceNote: 「鑽石手 / 紙手」HODL register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
網路上的人都說要當鑽石手，跌再多也要抱緊。
我抱緊了。
抱到後來才發現，我手裡的不是鑽石，是一塊一直在融化的冰。
現在我終於懂了：鑽石手跟死抱不放，差別只在於最後有沒有解套。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把『不停損』講成『鑽石手』的同溫層，貼在群組互相催眠
variations: 我以為我是鑽石手，結果只是手太僵，僵到不敢按賣出。
鑽石手的最高境界，是抱到那檔股票自己下市，幫你決定要不要賣。

en.content:
Everyone online says to have diamond hands — hold tight no matter how far it drops.
So I held tight.
Eventually I realized what's in my hands isn't a diamond, it's a block of ice that keeps melting.
Now I get it: diamond hands and refusing to let go are the same thing — the only difference is whether you ever break even.
en.editorNote: For the crowd that calls 'never cutting losses' having diamond hands. Share in the chat for mutual hypnosis
variations: I thought I had diamond hands. Turns out my hands were just too stiff to hit the sell button.
Peak diamond hands is holding until the stock delists itself and makes the decision for you.

items.sourceNote: 「市場是把錢從沒耐心轉給有耐心的人」反諷 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
書上說：市場是一個把錢從沒耐心的人，轉移到有耐心的人手上的工具。
我看完很感動，決定當那個有耐心的人。
結果我抱了五年，錢還是被轉走了。
後來我才懂，原來我有耐心，但我接手的對象，比我更有耐心。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把名言抄在筆記本第一頁、結果還是賠錢的長期投資者
variations: 我很有耐心，耐心地看著本金一年一年慢慢消失。
市場確實會獎勵有耐心的人，只是那個人從來不是我。

en.content:
The book says: the market is a device for transferring money from the impatient to the patient.
I was so moved I decided to be the patient one.
Five years later, my money still got transferred away.
Then I understood: I was patient — but whoever was on the other side was even more patient than me.
en.editorNote: For the long-term investor who copied that quote onto page one of their notebook and still lost money
variations: I'm very patient. Patiently watching my principal disappear a little more each year.
The market does reward the patient. That person has just never been me.

items.sourceNote: 「梗股 / 迷因股 FOMO」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
全網都在瘋一檔迷因股，論壇、限動、群組，連我媽都傳訊息問我。
我不想當最後一個知道的人，於是我衝了。
結果我不是最後一個知道的人，我是最後一個進場的人。
那檔股票把全網的笑話都講完之後，最後一個笑話留給了我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被迷因股熱潮 FOMO、結果接到最後一棒的跟風仔
variations: 迷因股的笑點我都看懂了，唯一沒看懂的是我自己也變成了笑點。
我以為我在玩梗，後來發現梗在玩我。

en.content:
The whole internet was hyping a meme stock — forums, stories, group chats, even my mom texted to ask about it.
I didn't want to be the last one to know, so I sent it.
Turns out I wasn't the last to know. I was the last to buy.
That stock told every joke on the internet, and saved the final punchline for me.
en.editorNote: For the trend-chaser who got FOMO'd into a meme stock and caught the very last bag
variations: I got every joke about the meme stock. The one I missed was that I had become one.
I thought I was in on the meme. Turns out the meme was in on me.

items.sourceNote: 「設停損但手動取消」紀律自欺 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我這次很有紀律，認真設了停損點。
股價跌到停損點的那一刻，我有更紀律的動作——把停損點往下移。
移了三次之後，我終於達成一個成就：一個永遠不會被觸發的停損點。
紀律的精髓，原來是對自己很有原則地破例。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在每次手指停在『取消委託』上方猶豫的那一秒提醒自己
variations: 我的停損點很會跑，每次股價快碰到，它就先閃了。
我設停損是為了心安，取消停損是為了帳面好看。

en.content:
This time I had discipline — I set a real stop-loss.
The instant the price hit it, I made an even more disciplined move: I dragged the stop lower.
Three drags later, I unlocked an achievement: a stop-loss that can never be triggered.
The essence of discipline, it turns out, is making exceptions for yourself with great principle.
en.editorNote: For the second your finger hovers over 'cancel order' — read this first
variations: My stop-loss is a great runner. Every time the price gets close, it bolts first.
I set the stop for peace of mind, then cancel it to keep the statement looking nice.

items.sourceNote: 「打開對帳單像開盲盒」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
現在打開對帳單，對我來說是一種開盲盒的體驗。
紅的、綠的、深綠的、墨綠到發黑的，每天都是驚喜。
唯一的差別是，別人的盲盒打開有東西，我的盲盒打開，是少一塊。
我終於擁有了一個收藏：滿滿一櫃的負數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個打開對帳單前要先深呼吸的同好，配收盤後的傍晚服用
variations: 我的對帳單比恐怖片還刺激，因為它是真的。
別人收集公仔，我收集套牢的成本價，而且越收越多。

en.content:
Opening my brokerage statement these days is basically a blind-box experience.
Red, green, dark green, green so deep it's nearly black — every day is a surprise.
The only difference is that other people's blind boxes contain something. Mine open to reveal one less chunk of money.
I've finally built a collection: a full cabinet of negative numbers.
en.editorNote: For everyone who takes a deep breath before opening their statement. Best taken in the evening after the close
variations: My account statement is scarier than a horror movie, because mine is real.
Other people collect figurines. I collect underwater cost-basis entries, and the collection keeps growing.

items.sourceNote: 台股破4萬卻虧損 / 報酬率-36% 套牢 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
大盤衝破四萬點那天，新聞標題寫著『全民同歡』。
我打開對帳單，報酬率負三十六，一個人在角落同悲。
原來指數創新高跟我有沒有賺錢，是兩件完全沒有關係的事。
大盤過它的四萬，我守我的負三十六，互不打擾。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個在大盤破關當天、自己卻還深套的同好，貼在群組互相取暖
variations: 指數的新高，是用我帳戶的新低換來的。
全民同歡的『全民』裡，查無此人。

en.content:
The day the index smashed through 40,000, the headline read 'everyone celebrates.'
I opened my statement — down 36 percent — and celebrated alone in the corner, sobbing.
Turns out a new all-time high on the index and whether I make money are two completely unrelated events.
The index keeps its 40,000, I keep my minus 36, and we leave each other in peace.
en.editorNote: For everyone deeply underwater on the day the index broke out. Share in the chat for mutual comfort
variations: The index's new high was paid for with the new low in my account.
Of all the 'everyone' supposedly celebrating, I am not on the list.

items.sourceNote: 「broker retirement plan / 退休計畫是券商的」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我跟券商一起規劃了一個退休計畫。
計畫進行得很順利，再過幾年應該就能達標。
唯一的小問題是：那個能順利退休的人，是我的營業員，不是我。
我每筆手續費，都是在替別人的夢想集點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在月底結算手續費、發現自己貢獻不少的時候自嘲
variations: 我努力交易了好多年，最後幫一個人達成了財務自由——可惜不是我。
我的本金是流動的，券商的業績是穩定的。

en.content:
My broker and I worked out a retirement plan together.
It's going great — a few more years and we should hit the target.
The only small catch: the person who gets to retire comfortably is my account rep, not me.
Every commission I pay is just collecting points toward somebody else's dream.
en.editorNote: For the end-of-month moment when you tally up just how much you contributed in fees
variations: I traded hard for years and finally helped someone reach financial freedom — sadly, not me.
My principal is volatile. My broker's bonus is stable.

items.sourceNote: 「market is bad but I slept like a baby — 每小時醒來哭一次」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人問我最近大盤這麼震盪，晚上睡得好嗎。
我說睡得跟嬰兒一樣。
他鬆了一口氣，說那很好啊。
我說對啊，跟嬰兒一樣，每隔一兩個小時就醒來，醒來就哭一次，哭完繼續睡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個在盤勢動盪期間，半夜會莫名驚醒看手機的同好
variations: 我睡得很安穩，安穩到每次閉眼前都會先看一次美股期貨。
別人數羊入睡，我數庫存損益，數著數著就清醒了。

en.content:
Someone asked if I've been sleeping okay with the market this choppy lately.
I said I sleep like a baby.
They looked relieved, said that's great.
I said yeah — just like a baby: I wake up every hour or two, cry a little, then go back to sleep.
en.editorNote: For anyone who jolts awake at 3 a.m. to check their phone during a volatile stretch
variations: I sleep peacefully — so peacefully that I check the overnight futures one last time before closing my eyes.
Other people count sheep to fall asleep. I count unrealized losses, and the counting wakes me right up.

items.sourceNote: 散戶三情緒『捨不得、不認輸、不甘心』register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
專家分析散戶為什麼總是賠錢，列了三個原因：捨不得、不認輸、不甘心。
我看完默默對號入座，三個全中。
捨不得停損，不認輸所以加碼，不甘心所以又凹回去。
別人是診斷報告，我這張是滿分成績單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個看散戶心理分析、結果發現在說自己的同好
variations: 專家說的散戶三大弱點，我把它活成了三大特長。
我不是看不懂這些道理，我是看懂了還是照樣犯。

en.content:
An expert broke down why retail traders always lose money, listing three reasons: can't let go, won't admit defeat, can't stand losing.
I read it and quietly checked every box. Three for three.
Can't let go, so I don't cut. Won't admit defeat, so I average down. Can't stand losing, so I dig back in.
For everyone else it's a diagnosis. For me it's a perfect-score report card.
en.editorNote: For everyone who reads a retail-investor psychology breakdown and realizes it's describing them
variations: The expert's three weaknesses of retail traders? I turned all three into core competencies.
It's not that I don't understand the lesson. I understand it and break the rule anyway.

items.sourceNote: PTT 股版「別人恐懼我貪婪，別人小虧我破產」名言反諷 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
巴菲特說：別人恐懼時我貪婪，別人貪婪時我恐懼。
我認真照做了。
別人小虧的時候，我貪婪地加碼。
別人停損出場的時候，我恐懼地不敢賣。
同一句話，我活成了完全相反的結果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把投資名言背得滾瓜爛熟、操作起來卻剛好相反的同好
variations: 投資金句我每句都會背，只是背完之後，照樣反著做。
別人恐懼我貪婪，別人小虧我破產，這句才是我的真實版本。

en.content:
Buffett said: be greedy when others are fearful, fearful when others are greedy.
I followed it faithfully.
When others took small losses, I got greedy and bought more.
When others cut and left, I got fearful and couldn't sell.
Same quote, and I lived out the exact opposite result.
en.editorNote: For everyone who has every investing quote memorized and still trades the inverse of it
variations: I can recite every famous investing quote. Then I do the opposite anyway.
Greedy when others fear, broke when others take a small loss — that's the real version for me.

items.sourceNote: PTT 股版「庫存綠到有牛在上面吃草」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我最近庫存還好嗎。
我說好得很，綠意盎然。
他說那不錯啊，環保。
我說對，綠到都可以放牛上去吃草了，再過陣子應該還能搭個帳篷露營。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個打開券商 APP，看到一整片綠油油的同好
variations: 我的對帳單比公園還綠，差別是公園讓人放鬆，我這個讓人血壓上升。
別人在陽台種花，我在帳戶裡種了一整片草原。

en.content:
A friend asked how my holdings were doing lately.
I said great, lush and green all over.
He said nice, very eco-friendly.
I said yeah, green enough to graze cattle on, and in a few weeks I might be able to pitch a tent and go camping.
en.editorNote: For everyone who opens the brokerage app to a wall of green
variations: My statement is greener than a public park. The difference is the park relaxes you and mine raises my blood pressure.
Other people grow flowers on the balcony. I grew an entire meadow inside my account.

items.sourceNote: 「沒賣就不算賠 / 我還沒輸而不是我正在輸」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我的投資哲學很簡單：只要沒按下賣出，就不算真的賠。
所以我帳面上的負三十趴，在我心裡只是『暫時還沒回來』。
用這套邏輯，我已經連續兩年沒有賠過任何一毛錢。
我不是沒在輸，我只是堅持告訴自己『我還沒輸』。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個用『沒賣就不算賠』催眠自己、實際上深套的同好
variations: 只要我不打開對帳單，我的資產就永遠是滿的。
帳面虧損不是虧損，是一種還沒兌現的樂觀。

en.content:
My investing philosophy is simple: as long as I don't hit sell, it's not a real loss.
So the minus 30 percent on my statement is, in my heart, just 'not back yet.'
With this logic, I haven't lost a single cent in two straight years.
I'm not losing. I'm just stubbornly telling myself 'I haven't lost yet.'
en.editorNote: For everyone deep underwater who hypnotizes themselves with 'it's not a loss until you sell'
variations: As long as I never open the statement, my assets are forever intact.
An unrealized loss isn't a loss. It's optimism that hasn't been cashed in.

items.sourceNote: 「How to make a small fortune: start with a large one」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人問我，要怎麼在股市裡賺到一筆小錢。
我說很簡單，先帶一筆大錢進來就好。
進場前我的存款是一大筆，現在剩一小筆。
你看，我成功了，完美達成『賺到一筆小錢』的目標。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個本金進場是一大筆、出場剩一小撮的同好
variations: 我在股市實現了財富的精簡化，從一大筆精簡到一小撮。
想把大錢變小錢，最快的方法就是相信自己的選股能力。

en.content:
Someone asked me how to make a small fortune in the stock market.
I said it's easy — just walk in with a large one first.
Before I entered, my savings were a big pile. Now they're a small pile.
See? I succeeded. Perfectly hit my target of 'making a small fortune.'
en.editorNote: For everyone who walked in with a big stack and walked out with a handful
variations: I achieved wealth minimalism in the market — downsized from a large fortune to a small one.
The fastest way to turn a big fortune into a small one is to trust your own stock-picking skills.

items.sourceNote: 「賣掉它就漲、不賣它就跌」散戶詛咒 / 反指標自嘲 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
群組裡有人問我看好哪一檔，他想跟。
我認真分析了半天，最後推薦了一檔我自己重押的。
隔天他傳訊息來：謝謝，我反著做，賺爛了。
原來我在這個市場最大的價值，是當一個穩定的反指標。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被同好當成反指標、講什麼大家就反著做的同好
variations: 想知道一檔股票會不會漲，問我就對了，然後反著做。
我看好的標的，是市場最可靠的賣出訊號。

en.content:
Someone in the group chat asked which stock I liked, said he wanted to follow me in.
I analyzed it carefully and recommended one I was heavily long.
Next day he messaged: thanks, I faded you and made a killing.
Turns out my greatest value in this market is being a reliable contrarian indicator.
en.editorNote: For everyone whose friends treat them as a fade signal — whatever they call, do the opposite
variations: Want to know if a stock will pump? Ask me, then do the opposite.
The names I'm bullish on are the market's most reliable sell signal.

items.sourceNote: 「他3萬9全賣傻眼台股到底在漲什麼」踏空 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我等了一整年，終於忍到回檔，把手上的股票全部出清，自認逃過一劫。
結果隔週大盤一路狂飆，再也沒回頭過。
現在我每天打開盤面，第一句話都是：到底在漲什麼？
第二句話是：為什麼漲的時候我都不在場上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個自以為躲過下跌、結果躲過了一整段大行情的踏空族
variations: 我不是被套牢，我是被空手套牢，套在場外動彈不得。
我清倉的那天，就是這波行情的起漲日，準到可以當公告。

en.content:
I waited the whole year, finally held out for a pullback, dumped every share I owned, and figured I'd dodged a bullet.
The next week the market took off and never looked back.
Now every time I open the screen, my first line is: what is even going up?
My second line is: why am I never in the market when it does.
en.editorNote: For everyone who thought they dodged the drop and instead dodged the entire rally
variations: I'm not trapped in a position. I'm trapped on the sidelines, frozen in cash.
The day I went all-cash was the exact launch day of this rally — accurate enough to publish as an announcement.

items.sourceNote: 「committed long term relationship with index funds, individual equities broke my heart」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我現在只跟指數型 ETF 認真交往。
不是我變專情，是個股傷我太多次。
每一檔我重押過的個股，都教會我一件事：原來心可以這樣被砍到停損。
談感情跟買股票一樣，最後我選擇了一個不會讓我半夜驚醒的對象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被個股傷透心、最後投奔大盤 ETF 懷抱的同好
variations: 個股是初戀，刻骨銘心，賠光收場；ETF 是現任，平淡，但至少還在。
我跟個股分手的原因很單純：它從來沒有想過要對我負責。

en.content:
These days I'm only in a serious relationship with index ETFs.
It's not that I got loyal — individual stocks just hurt me too many times.
Every single name I went heavy on taught me the same thing: a heart really can get stopped out.
Dating is like buying stocks. In the end I picked the one that doesn't jolt me awake at 3 a.m.
en.editorNote: For everyone who got their heart broken by single stocks and ran into the arms of a broad-market ETF
variations: Individual stocks were the first love — unforgettable, and ended in total loss. The ETF is the current partner: dull, but still here.
I broke up with single stocks for one simple reason: they never once intended to be accountable to me.

items.sourceNote: 「梭哈 / 關燈吃麵」散戶梗 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
上週我看好一檔，信心爆棚，直接梭哈。
這週我關燈吃麵。
不是停電，是我捨不得開燈看對帳單；也不是想吃麵，是預算只剩下麵。
梭哈到關燈吃麵，中間只隔了三個交易日，效率高得驚人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個一時衝動全押、隔幾天就默默改吃泡麵的同好
variations: 梭哈的盡頭不是財富自由，是把冰箱裡的存糧自由地吃完。
我不是在過極簡生活，我是被一檔股票強迫極簡。

en.content:
Last week I loved a name, confidence through the roof, so I went all in.
This week I'm eating instant noodles in the dark.
The lights aren't out from a blackout — I just can't bear to switch them on and see the statement. And I'm not eating noodles by choice — noodles are all the budget allows.
From all-in to noodles-in-the-dark took exactly three trading days. Frighteningly efficient.
en.editorNote: For everyone who YOLO'd a position on impulse and quietly downgraded to instant noodles days later
variations: The end of going all in isn't financial freedom. It's freely finishing whatever's left in the pantry.
I'm not living a minimalist life. A single stock forced minimalism on me.

items.sourceNote: 「asked my stocks for a dividend, they said we're just here for the exposure」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我跟我的持股要求配個股利，意思意思就好。
它們很有默契地回我：抱歉，我們只是來這裡曝光，不負責賺錢。
我才發現，我養的不是金雞母，是一群來蹭版面的網紅，光鮮亮麗，零收入。
存股存了三年，存到一櫃子只會出現在新聞標題、不會出現在我戶頭的明星股。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個滿手熱門題材股、卻一毛股利都沒看到的存股族
variations: 我的持股很會上新聞，很不會進我的口袋。
別人存股是養金雞母，我存股是養一群只會擺拍的網美。

en.content:
I asked my holdings to throw me a dividend, just a token amount.
In perfect unison they replied: sorry, we're only here for the exposure, we don't do income.
That's when I realized I wasn't raising golden geese — I was raising a flock of influencers who showed up for the spotlight: glamorous, zero revenue.
Three years of accumulating shares, and all I collected was a cabinet of celebrity stocks that appear in headlines but never in my account.
en.editorNote: For the dividend collector holding a stack of hot-theme stocks that have never paid out a cent
variations: My holdings are great at making the news and terrible at making it into my wallet.
Other people accumulate shares to raise golden geese. I accumulated a flock of influencers who only know how to pose.

items.sourceNote: 2026 馬年「馬倒成功 / 哭哭馬 喪萌」流行語 + 投資自嘲混搭 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
馬年大家都祝彼此馬到成功，我看著對帳單，只想到馬倒成功。
別人新年願望是發大財，我的新年願望是回本就好。
我現在的心情，就跟那隻爆紅的哭哭馬玩偶一樣：表情很委屈，但還是得繼續站在原地被市場摸頭。
喪萌的最高境界，就是一邊賠錢一邊覺得自己賠得很可愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個馬年開紅盤滿懷希望、結果年初就先被洗一波的同好
variations: 今年我沒有馬到成功，但我成功地馬倒了，倒得很徹底。
我的投資組合就是一隻哭哭馬，表情委屈，但還是得撐著。

en.content:
It's the Year of the Horse, and everyone's wishing each other a galloping success. I look at my statement and all I can think of is a galloping faceplant.
Other people's New Year wish is to strike it rich. Mine is just to break even.
Right now I feel exactly like that viral crying-horse plushie: a thoroughly aggrieved expression, yet still forced to stand there and let the market pat me on the head.
Peak 'sad-cute' is losing money while finding yourself adorable for losing it.
en.editorNote: For everyone who opened the Year of the Horse full of hope and got flushed in the first week
variations: This year I didn't gallop to success. I galloped face-first into the ground — thoroughly.
My portfolio is basically that crying-horse plushie: aggrieved expression, still has to hang in there.

items.sourceNote: 台股破45000點、融資飆5726億、散戶開槓桿『不掉隊』register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
看到大家都在賺，我怕掉隊，於是開了融資。
別人說槓桿能放大獲利，這句話只對了一半——它確實放大了，放大的是我的虧損和我的心跳。
現在我不掉隊了，因為我跟我的本金，一起被甩在後面。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個怕落後而開槓桿、結果被放大教訓的同好
variations: 槓桿是把雙面刃，我很厲害，每次都精準握住刀刃那一邊。
我以為融資是上車，後來發現是上了一台沒有煞車的車。

en.content:
Everyone was making money, I was scared of falling behind, so I opened a margin account.
They say leverage amplifies your gains — that's only half true. It really did amplify something: my losses and my heart rate.
Now I'm not falling behind anymore, because me and my principal got left behind together.
en.editorNote: For everyone who levered up out of FOMO and got the lesson amplified instead
variations: Leverage is a double-edged sword. I'm so skilled I grab the blade side every single time.
I thought margin was getting on the train. Turns out it was a train with no brakes.

items.sourceNote: 「401k now a 201k / great retirement plan」退休帳戶縮水 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我本來幫自己準備了一筆退休金，金額很漂亮。
經過這幾年的英明操作，它現在還是很漂亮，只是少了一個零。
別人的退休計畫是越存越多，我的退休計畫是越存越短。
照這個進度，我的退休金再過幾年，就能精簡到一個整數位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個眼看退休帳戶一年比一年瘦的同好
variations: 我的退休金正在朝『提早歸零』穩定邁進。
我沒有把退休金花掉，我只是把它投資到一個再也找不回來的地方。

en.content:
I'd set aside a retirement fund for myself, and the number looked great.
After a few years of my brilliant trading, it still looks great — just missing one zero.
Other people's retirement plan is to save more and more. Mine is to save less and less.
At this rate, in a few more years my retirement fund will be streamlined down to a single digit.
en.editorNote: For everyone watching their retirement account get thinner year after year
variations: My retirement fund is making steady progress toward 'early zero.'
I didn't spend my retirement savings. I just invested them somewhere I can never find again.

items.sourceNote: 「risk tolerance so high my portfolio is now a non-profit」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
理專幫我做了風險屬性評估，看完之後他露出佩服的眼神。
他說：先生，您的風險承受度高到一個境界，您的投資組合已經不能算投資了，比較接近做公益。
我問他公益對象是誰，他笑而不答。
後來我才懂，受益單位是市場，捐款人是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個高風險高承受、結果默默做了一整年公益的同好
variations: 我的投資組合是非營利的，而且非常徹底，連我自己都沒營利。
風險承受度測驗我拿了滿分，獎品是把本金捐出去。

en.content:
My advisor ran a risk-tolerance assessment, and afterward he gave me a look of genuine admiration.
He said: sir, your risk tolerance has reached such a level that your portfolio can no longer be called investing — it's closer to charity work.
I asked who the beneficiary was. He just smiled and said nothing.
Later I figured it out: the beneficiary is the market, and the donor is me.
en.editorNote: For the high-risk-tolerance type who quietly ran a year-long charity operation
variations: My portfolio is non-profit, and very thoroughly so — even I didn't profit.
I aced the risk-tolerance quiz. The prize was getting to donate my principal.

items.sourceNote: 散戶『處分效果』：太早賣賺的、死抱賠的 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我有一套很穩定的操作模式：賺一點點就趕快賣，怕它跌回去；賠很多就死抱著，等它漲回來。
結果賺的那些，賣掉之後繼續飆；賠的那些，抱著抱著就下市了。
專家把這叫做處分效果，我把這叫做：把好的留給市場，把壞的留給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個賺小錢就跑、賠大錢死撐的同好，貼在群組互相提醒
variations: 我停利的速度跟我停損的速度，剛好相反，這就是我穩定賠錢的秘訣。
我手上會留下來的，永遠是賠錢的那幾檔，因為賺的早就被我嚇跑了。

en.content:
I have a very consistent trading style: sell the second I'm up a little, scared it'll drop back; cling on when I'm down a lot, waiting for it to come back.
The ones I sold kept ripping after I sold them. The ones I clung to clung right into delisting.
Experts call it the disposition effect. I call it: keep the good ones for the market, keep the bad ones for myself.
en.editorNote: For everyone who runs from small gains and white-knuckles big losses — share in the chat as a reminder
variations: My speed taking profits and my speed taking losses are exact opposites. That's the secret to my consistent losing.
The only names left in my account are the losers, because I scared all the winners off long ago.

items.sourceNote: 台股站上4萬、千金股飆 + FOMO『不掉隊』register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
現在隨便一檔熱門股都好幾百塊，動不動就千金股，我看著看著就焦慮了。
為了證明自己跟得上潮流，我咬牙買了一張最貴的那檔。
買完我終於體會到什麼叫千金難買早知道——早知道我就不買了。
現在我手上有一張千金股，和一個千金難換的教訓。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個為了不掉隊硬買千金股、結果套在山頂的同好
variations: 千金股的『千金』不是指股價，是指我為它流的眼淚有多值錢。
我終於擁有一張別人口中的飆股，可惜它在我手上學會了走路要慢慢來。

en.content:
These days every hot stock costs hundreds a share, four-digit price tags everywhere, and watching it just made me anxious.
To prove I could keep up with the trend, I gritted my teeth and bought one share of the priciest name out there.
Right after buying, I finally understood the saying that hindsight is priceless — if I'd had it, I wouldn't have bought.
Now I own one ultra-expensive share, and one lesson money can't buy back.
en.editorNote: For everyone who bought a four-figure stock just to keep up and ended up stuck at the peak
variations: The 'priceless' part of a priceless stock isn't the share price — it's how valuable the tears I cried over it are.
I finally own one of those high-flyers everyone talks about. Shame it learned to take things slow the moment it landed in my account.

items.sourceNote: 高股息 ETF 殖利率回落、賺了股息賠了價差 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我買高股息 ETF 是為了領那筆漂亮的配息，每個月都期待入帳的那一天。
配息確實準時來了，一毛都沒少。
問題是除息那天，淨值也準時掉下去了，掉的金額剛好等於我領到的。
我終於懂了：所謂的高股息，是把我自己的錢，左手換到右手，還要先扣稅。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個興沖沖領了配息、回頭一看價差倒賠的存股族
variations: 我領到的不是被動收入，是我本金的零錢回饋。
高股息 ETF 教會我一件事：股息是真的，報酬是假的。

en.content:
I bought a high-dividend ETF for that beautiful payout, looking forward to deposit day every month.
The dividend showed up right on time, not a cent short.
The catch is, on ex-dividend day the NAV dropped right on time too — by exactly the amount I'd just received.
Now I get it: 'high dividend' means moving my own money from my left hand to my right, minus a withholding tax.
en.editorNote: For the dividend stacker who happily collected a payout and then noticed the capital loss underneath
variations: What I collected wasn't passive income. It was spare-change cashback on my own principal.
High-dividend ETFs taught me one thing: the dividend is real, the return is not.

items.sourceNote: 資訊落差 / 大戶出貨散戶接刀 心理學 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我終於想通主力跟散戶的差別在哪了。
主力知道什麼時候該進、什麼時候該出；我只知道什麼時候該後悔。
他們在資訊的最前面，我在新聞的最後面，等利多見報的那天，剛好就是我進場的那天。
原來我不是在投資，我是在幫一篇利多新聞，提供一個完美的結局。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個看到利多才衝進場、結果剛好接到最後一棒的同好
variations: 利多見報那一刻，不是起漲訊號，是我該逃命的訊號，只是我每次都看反。
我跟主力的時間軸差了三天，剛好夠他出貨、夠我套牢。

en.content:
I finally figured out the difference between the whales and us retail folks.
They know when to get in and when to get out. I only know when to regret.
They're at the front of the information; I'm at the tail end of the news. The day the good headline drops is the exact day I buy in.
Turns out I'm not investing. I'm providing a bullish news story with the perfect ending.
en.editorNote: For everyone who rushed in on a bullish headline and caught the very last bag
variations: The moment good news hits the press isn't a buy signal. It's my run-for-your-life signal — I just always read it backwards.
My timeline runs three days behind the whales': exactly enough for them to exit and me to get stuck.

items.sourceNote: 「portfolio has trust issues / buy high cry later」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我跟我的投資組合之間，現在有嚴重的信任問題。
我相信它會漲，它讓我失望；我相信它會反彈，它又讓我失望。
到最後我學乖了，我什麼都不相信，它就開始漲了。
原來這段關係維持的唯一方法，是我完全不抱期待，它才願意對我好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個跟自己庫存陷入信任危機、不敢再相信反彈的同好
variations: 我對我的庫存已經沒有期待了，只剩下習慣性的失望。
高點買進，低點哭泣，這就是我跟市場最穩定的相處模式。

en.content:
My portfolio and I have serious trust issues now.
I trusted it to go up — it let me down. I trusted it to bounce — it let me down again.
Eventually I wised up and stopped believing in anything, and that's when it finally rallied.
Turns out the only way this relationship survives is if I expect nothing at all — then it agrees to treat me well.
en.editorNote: For everyone in a trust crisis with their own holdings, too burned to believe in a bounce
variations: I have no expectations left for my holdings — just a habitual sense of disappointment.
Buy high, cry later. That's the most stable relationship I've ever had with the market.

items.sourceNote: 台股4萬點 FOMO『跟單存股 KOL』register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我追蹤了一個很厲害的存股 KOL，他曬對帳單，獲利幾百趴，看得我熱血沸騰。
我把他每一檔持股都照抄一遍，連張數比例都一模一樣。
結果他繼續賺，我開始賠。
後來我才懂：他曬的是三年前買的成本，我抄的是今天的價格，我們抄的根本不是同一張對帳單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個照抄網紅持股、卻買在今天高點的跟單族
variations: 我跟 KOL 買的是同一檔股票，唯一不同的是成本價差了一個山頭。
他曬的是結果，我抄的是入場券，價錢還比較貴。

en.content:
I followed this brilliant dividend-stacking influencer who posts his statements — hundreds of percent in gains, it got my blood pumping.
I copied every single one of his holdings, matched the exact ratios, share for share.
He kept winning. I started losing.
Then it hit me: he's showing the cost from three years ago, and I'm copying today's price. We were never looking at the same statement.
en.editorNote: For everyone who mirrored an influencer's holdings but bought at today's top
variations: The influencer and I own the same stock. The only difference is our cost basis is a whole mountain apart.
He's showing the result; I copied the entry ticket — and paid more for it.

items.sourceNote: 「invested in my own failure / better luck」自嘲 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人說，如果你做什麼都失敗，那不如投資自己。
我聽完覺得很有道理，於是我把錢投進了股市，畢竟操盤的也是我自己。
結果證明這個邏輯非常一致：我做什麼都失敗，包括投資我自己。
現在我終於找到一個穩定的標的了，那就是我自己的判斷，穩定地錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個自認是反指標、連投資自己都會賠的同好
variations: 我這輩子最一致的，就是我的判斷，一致地站在錯的那一邊。
投資自己沒有錯，錯的是那個被投資的我。

en.content:
Someone said if you fail at everything, you should just invest in yourself.
Made total sense to me, so I put my money in the market — after all, I'm the one trading it.
Turns out the logic is remarkably consistent: I fail at everything, including investing in myself.
I've finally found one stable asset: my own judgment, stably wrong.
en.editorNote: For everyone who is their own contrarian indicator and loses even when betting on themselves
variations: The most consistent thing in my life is my judgment — consistently on the wrong side.
There's nothing wrong with investing in yourself. The problem is the self being invested in.

items.sourceNote: 「跌一點就砍、漲回來又追高，這才是標準韭菜」洗盤被洗出 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
市場只是輕輕抖了一下，我就嚇得把單砍了。
它漲回來，我又怕錯過，追了上去。
抖一下我跑，漲回來我追，這套動作我重複了一整年，動作越來越熟練，本金越來越乾淨。
後來我才懂，主力不是在跟我搶股票，是在跟我玩一二三木頭人，而我每次都動。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被一根黑K嚇出場、又被一根紅K騙回來的同好
variations: 我不是被套牢洗出去的，我是自己抖出去的。
主力洗盤的成本很低，因為只要嚇一下，我就會自己跳車。

en.content:
The market gave one little shake, and I panic-sold.
It climbed back, and I chased it so I wouldn't miss out.
Shake, I bail. Bounce, I chase. I ran this routine for a whole year — smoother every time, account cleaner every time.
Then it hit me: the whales aren't fighting me for shares. They're playing red-light-green-light, and I move every single round.
en.editorNote: For everyone scared out by a single red candle and lured back by a single green one
variations: I wasn't washed out by getting trapped. I shook myself out.
Shaking me out is cheap for the whales — one little scare and I jump off the train myself.

items.sourceNote: PTT「別人恐懼我貪婪，別人小虧我破產」幹話語錄 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我把那句名言奉為信仰：別人恐懼，我貪婪。
於是別人恐懼的時候，我貪婪地全押。
別人後來小虧，我破產。
我終於參透這句話的真義：原來貪婪跟勇敢只差一個結果，而我每次都領到那個比較難看的結果。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把投資名言貼在牆上、結果活成反例的同好
variations: 別人恐懼我貪婪，別人停損我加碼，別人解套我畢業。
名言沒有騙我，只是它沒說那個貪婪的人最後會是接刀的那個。

en.content:
I treated that famous line like scripture: be greedy when others are fearful.
So when everyone got scared, I greedily went all in.
They took a small loss. I went bankrupt.
Now I finally grasp what the quote really means: greedy and brave are separated by one thing — the outcome — and I always draw the uglier one.
en.editorNote: For everyone who taped an investing quote to the wall and became its cautionary example
variations: Greedy when others fear, averaging down when others cut, graduating when others break even.
The quote didn't lie to me. It just left out that the greedy one ends up catching the falling knife.

items.sourceNote: 「lost his balance / 交易員破產因為失去平衡」雙關 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我最近在學什麼，我說我在練平衡。
他以為我去學瑜伽，其實我是在學怎麼讓帳戶收支平衡。
練到現在，我的帳戶確實很平衡了——進帳是零，餘額也快是零，完美對稱。
原來達成財務平衡最快的方法，是把錢全部賠光，剩下的零，最平衡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在月底結算發現帳戶見底時拿來苦中作樂
variations: 我終於達成收支平衡了，因為兩邊都是零。
別人靠存錢平衡，我靠賠光平衡，殊途同歸。

en.content:
A friend asked what I've been practicing lately. I said I'm working on my balance.
He assumed I took up yoga. Really I'm learning how to balance my account.
And it's working — my account is perfectly balanced now: zero coming in, balance nearly zero too. Flawless symmetry.
Turns out the fastest way to financial balance is to lose it all, because the zero that's left is the most balanced number there is.
en.editorNote: For the end-of-month moment when you tally up and find the account scraping bottom
variations: I finally balanced my budget — both sides are zero.
Other people balance their finances by saving. I balance mine by losing it all. Same destination.

items.sourceNote: 「38萬散戶進場後暴跌、新進散戶住套房」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說這波有幾十萬新散戶進場。
我就是那幾十萬之一，懷著滿腔熱血，在最熱鬧的那天報到。
報到完隔天，大盤就送了我一份見面禮，直接讓我住進套房。
別人買房要存頭期款，我什麼都沒準備，市場一個禮拜就幫我安排好了一間，還免費附帶長期租約。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個剛開戶就遇到回檔、直接住進套房的新手同好
variations: 我進股市的第一個成就，不是獲利，是入住。
別人是來投資的，我是來市場辦租屋手續的，租期未定。

en.content:
The news said a few hundred thousand new retail traders just entered the market.
I'm one of those hundred thousands — full of fire, signing in on the busiest day of all.
The morning after I signed in, the index handed me a welcome gift and moved me straight into the underwater suite.
Other people save for a down payment to get a place. I prepped nothing, and within a week the market arranged a unit for me — lease term: indefinite.
en.editorNote: For the rookie who opened an account, hit a pullback immediately, and moved straight into the underwater suite
variations: My first achievement in the stock market wasn't a profit. It was a move-in.
Other people came to invest. I came to sign a lease — term still undetermined.

items.sourceNote: 名嘴喊「回檔是機會」反引恐慌 / 反指標 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
名嘴在電視上喊：「回檔就是機會，現在不買更待何時。」
我全村的希望都壓上去了。
結果隔天綠油油，原來他講的機會，是給別人賣給我的機會。
現在我看財經節目的方法很簡單：他喊買我就跑，他喊跑我就買，從此再也沒虧過——因為我已經沒錢了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把名嘴當反指標、結果發現自己才是真正反指標的同好
variations: 名嘴不是不準，他超準，只是準在我的反方向。
我終於找到一個穩賺的策略：聽名嘴的話，然後反著做。可惜我都是聽完才想到。

en.content:
The TV pundit shouted: 'A pullback is opportunity — if not now, when?'
I put the whole village's hopes on it.
Next day, blood-green everywhere. Turns out the opportunity he meant was the chance for someone to sell it to me.
Now my method for watching finance shows is simple: he says buy, I run; he says run, I buy. Haven't lost since — because I'm out of money.
en.editorNote: For everyone who treats pundits as a reverse indicator, only to discover they're the real reverse indicator
variations: The pundit isn't wrong. He's incredibly accurate — accurate in the exact opposite direction of me.
I finally found a guaranteed strategy: hear the pundit out, then do the opposite. Shame I only think of it afterward.

items.sourceNote: 「I bought the dip and now I live there」雙關 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
他們叫我逢低買進，我照做了。
我買了那個低點，然後就再也沒離開過。
別人逢低買進是把它當折扣，我逢低買進是把它當新家，買完直接搬進去住。
現在這個低點是我的戶籍地，水電都繳了，連郵差都知道我住這。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個逢低買進、結果直接定居在低點的長期住戶
variations: 我買的不是低點，是房子，因為我之後就一直住在裡面了。
逢低買進的副作用是：你會跟那個低點建立一段長期關係。

en.content:
They told me to buy the dip, so I did.
I bought that dip, and I never left.
For other people buying the dip is grabbing a discount. For me it's buying a new home — I moved straight in.
This dip is my registered address now. Utilities are paid, and even the mailman knows I live here.
en.editorNote: For everyone who bought the dip and became a permanent resident of it
variations: I didn't buy a dip. I bought a house, because I've been living in it ever since.
A side effect of buying the dip: you build a long-term relationship with that dip.

items.sourceNote: 「diversified portfolio: 50% stress, 50% regret」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
理專一直叫我要分散投資，不要把雞蛋放同一個籃子。
我聽話，認真分散了。
現在我的投資組合配置非常均衡：一半是壓力，一半是後悔，兩邊完美對沖。
唯一沒分散到的，是虧損——那個很集中，全部都在我這。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在被理專勸『要分散風險』、結果分散完還是賠的時候自嘲
variations: 我的資產配置很科學：恐慌、焦慮、悔不當初，三大類股齊跌。
別人分散投資是為了降低風險，我分散投資是為了把難過分裝在不同帳戶。

en.content:
My advisor kept telling me to diversify — don't put all your eggs in one basket.
I listened. I diversified hard.
My portfolio is beautifully balanced now: 50% stress, 50% regret, the two perfectly hedged against each other.
The one thing I forgot to diversify is the losses — those are highly concentrated, all right here with me.
en.editorNote: For when your advisor preaches 'spread the risk' and you spread it and still lose
variations: My asset allocation is very scientific: panic, anxiety, and deep regret — three sectors, all down.
Other people diversify to lower risk. I diversify to spread my sadness across multiple accounts.

items.sourceNote: PTT「庫存綠到有牛在上面吃草」幹話語錄 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人問我庫存還好嗎，我說綠意盎然，生機勃勃。
他以為我在說 ESG 永續概念股，其實我是說我的損益。
我的庫存綠到一個程度，已經可以放牛上去吃草了，再過陣子大概能開放露營。
別人投資是為了財富自由，我投資是不小心經營了一片草原。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個打開庫存只看到一整片綠的同好，貼在群組互相施肥
variations: 我的對帳單已經不是股票了，是一座生態保護區。
綠色代表希望，所以我的帳戶充滿希望，滿到溢出來。

en.content:
Someone asked how my holdings were doing. I said lush and thriving, full of life.
They assumed I meant some ESG green-energy play. I meant my P&L.
My portfolio is so green you could let cattle graze on it. Give it a bit longer and I'll open it up for camping.
Other people invest for financial freedom. I accidentally became the steward of a meadow.
en.editorNote: For everyone whose holdings page is just a wall of green. Share in the chat and fertilize together
variations: My statement isn't a stock portfolio anymore. It's a nature preserve.
Green is the color of hope, so my account is overflowing with it.

items.sourceNote: 2026 多起當沖違約交割破億 / 槓桿開太大 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說今年違約交割案一件比一件大，最高一筆破億。
我看完很有共鳴，雖然我的數字後面少了好幾個零。
別人是當沖大戶玩到調不出錢，我是當沖小散玩到調不出便當錢。
我們的差別只是金額，那份『以為今天一定賺』的自信，是一模一樣的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個槓桿開太大、結算日才發現現金不夠的當沖同好
variations: 違約交割的起點，都是一句『反正當天就賣掉了』。
我跟破億大戶唯一的共同點，是我們都高估了自己的對帳單。

en.content:
The news says this year's settlement-default cases keep getting bigger, the biggest one over a hundred million.
I related hard — even though my number is missing a few zeros.
The big day-traders blow up when they can't scrape together the cash. I'm the small one who can't scrape together lunch money.
The only difference is the amount. That 'today's definitely a winner' confidence is exactly the same.
en.editorNote: For the day-trader who leveraged too hard and found out on settlement day the cash wasn't there
variations: Every settlement default starts with the words 'I'll just sell it before close anyway.'
The only thing I share with the hundred-million blow-up is that we both overestimated our statement.

items.sourceNote: 2026 殖利率從 4.5% 滑到 1.5%~2.5% / 存股族的崩潰 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
幾年前我開始存股，是因為殖利率有五趴，比定存香多了。
今年我打開來看，殖利率剩一點五趴，比定存還可憐。
我存的不是股，是一段越來越薄的感情。
當初為了現金流進場，現在現金流變成涓涓細流，本金還順便縮水，這叫做進可攻、退也守不住。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個當年衝著高殖利率存股、結果殖利率一路縮水的存股族
variations: 我的殖利率跟我的髮量一樣，每年都在默默變少。
存股存到後來，配的息還不夠付我看盤喝的咖啡。

en.content:
Years ago I started building a dividend portfolio because the yield was 5% — way sweeter than a savings account.
This year I opened it up: the yield is down to 1.5%, sadder than a savings account.
What I've been stacking isn't stock — it's a relationship that gets thinner every year.
I came in for the cash flow; now the cash flow is a trickle and the principal shrank too. They call that all upside, no downside protection.
en.editorNote: For the dividend investor who piled in for the high yield and watched it bleed lower every year
variations: My dividend yield is like my hairline — quietly thinning a little more each year.
By now the dividends don't even cover the coffee I drink while watching the screen.

items.sourceNote: 2026 假投資群組 / 冒名投顧詐騙 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人把我拉進一個『穩賺老師帶單群』，群裡每個人天天截圖獲利，氣氛熱絡到不行。
我心想終於遇到貴人，跟著進場。
後來才發現，群裡九十九個人都是同一個人，唯一的真人散戶就是我。
老師確實帶我賺到了人生第一桶金——可惜那桶金，是流向他的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個差點被『保證獲利帶單群』騙進去的同好，分享前提醒大家別點陌生連結
variations: 凡是群組裡每個人都在賺，只有你還沒進場，那個要被收割的，通常就是你。
保證獲利的『保證』是真的，只是受益人欄位寫的不是我。

en.content:
Someone pulled me into a 'guaranteed-win signal group' where everyone posted profit screenshots all day. The energy was electric.
I thought I'd finally found my lucky break and followed them in.
Later I realized all ninety-nine members were the same person. The only real retail trader was me.
The 'teacher' really did help me make my first pot of gold — it just flowed straight into his pocket.
en.editorNote: For anyone who almost got reeled into a 'guaranteed-profit signal group.' Remind people not to click strange links before sharing
variations: If everyone in the chat is winning and you're the only one not in yet, you're the one about to get harvested.
The 'guarantee' in guaranteed profit is real. The beneficiary line just doesn't say my name.

items.sourceNote: 外資期貨空單 6.7 萬口創新高 / 散戶當最後逃命波 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說外資空單來到六萬七千口，創歷史新高。
我看不太懂期貨，但我看得懂一件事：聰明錢都在準備落跑。
結果我這個看不懂的，反而在最高點奮勇進場，當了那道大家口中的『最後逃命波』。
別人逃命，我逆向衝鋒，原來我不是散戶，我是替別人擋子彈的志工。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在外資狂砍、自己卻還想攤平的盤中，提醒自己誰才是聰明錢
variations: 外資布的空單，最後都需要有人來填，那個人剛好就坐在我的位子。
大家都在找出口的時候，我從入口走了進來。

en.content:
The news says foreign funds are now holding 67,000 short contracts, an all-time record.
I don't really get futures, but I get one thing: the smart money is packing to leave.
So naturally, the guy who doesn't get it charged in at the very top, becoming what everyone calls the 'final escape wave.'
Others flee, I counter-charge. Turns out I'm not a retail trader — I'm a volunteer taking the bullets for everyone else.
en.editorNote: For the intraday moment when foreigners are dumping and you still want to average down — remember who the smart money is
variations: Every short the big funds build eventually needs someone to fill it. That someone happens to sit in my seat.
While everyone was looking for the exit, I walked in through the entrance.

items.sourceNote: 「researching investing instead of sleeping responsibly」熬夜做功課 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，正常人在睡覺，我在研究一檔我根本不會買的股票的第七份財報。
我跟自己說，這叫做用功，這叫做投資人的自我修養。
隔天我頂著黑眼圈上班，那檔我研究到天亮的股票，我最後沒買，買了另一檔當下衝動下單的——然後賠了。
原來我熬的不是夜，是把該睡的覺，拿去交學費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個半夜不睡覺、熬夜做功課結果還是憑衝動下單的認真仔
variations: 我研究股票的時間，遠遠超過我願意拿來睡覺的時間，成果則完全相反。
正常人熬夜是為了趕報告，我熬夜是為了趕在天亮前說服自己這檔會漲。

en.content:
3 a.m. Normal people are asleep. I'm reading the seventh earnings report of a stock I'm never going to buy.
I tell myself this is diligence, this is the discipline of a serious investor.
Next day I show up to work with raccoon eyes. The stock I researched till sunrise? Didn't buy it. Bought a different one on a pure impulse instead — and lost.
Turns out I wasn't pulling an all-nighter. I was trading the sleep I owed myself for tuition.
en.editorNote: For the diligent type who skips sleep to do homework and still places the order on impulse
variations: I spend far more time researching stocks than I'm willing to spend sleeping. The results are the exact opposite.
Normal people stay up to finish a report. I stay up to convince myself, before sunrise, that this one will go up.

items.sourceNote: 2026 題材股本益比數十倍 / 散戶以『未來 AI 成長』為由追高 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我買的那檔股票本益比五十倍，朋友說太貴了。
我說你不懂，這是在買它的未來。
半年後它跌回十倍，我才發現，原來我買的不是未來，是別人已經先賺走的過去。
所謂『買成長性』，翻譯成白話就是：用十年後的好消息，付今天追高的帳單。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個用『買未來』說服自己追高本益比題材股的同好
variations: 本益比五十倍的意思是，公司要連續賺五十年，我才剛好回本。
我不是買貴，我是買得很有遠見——遠到本金都看不見了。

en.content:
The stock I bought trades at fifty times earnings. My friend said it was too expensive.
I told him he didn't get it — I was buying its future.
Six months later it fell back to ten times, and I realized I hadn't bought the future. I'd bought a past that someone smarter had already cashed out of.
'Buying growth,' translated into plain language, means paying today's top-chasing bill with good news from ten years out.
en.editorNote: For everyone who talks themselves into a sky-high-multiple hype stock by calling it 'buying the future'
variations: A 50x P/E means the company has to print money for fifty straight years before I break even.
I didn't overpay. I bought with great foresight — so far ahead I can no longer see my principal.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Line 群組 / 名嘴帶風向 利多利空謠言 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
群組裡的名嘴喊：這檔利多滿天飛，明天起漲。
我隔天衝進去，它跌停。
同一個名嘴改口：這叫洗盤，洗完才會噴。
洗了一個月，洗掉的不是浮額，是我的本金。
後來我才懂，名嘴從來沒說錯，因為不管漲跌，他都有一套說法在等我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在又被群組『老師看法』推坑後，提醒自己誰在帶風向
variations: 名嘴的話永遠是對的，因為漲了叫看對，跌了叫洗盤。
他帶的不是風向，是我的單。

en.content:
The pundit in the group chat shouts: this one's flooded with good news, it rips tomorrow.
I charge in the next day. Limit down.
Same pundit, new line: that's a shakeout, it only moons after it shakes.
It shook for a month. What got shaken out wasn't the weak hands — it was my principal.
Then I understood: the pundit is never wrong, because whether it goes up or down, he's already got a story waiting for me.
en.editorNote: For the moment you get talked into a position by 'the teacher's take' again — remember who's steering the narrative
variations: The pundit is always right: if it goes up, he called it; if it drops, it's a shakeout.
He wasn't steering the narrative. He was steering my order.

items.sourceNote: 「make a small fortune on Wall Street — start with a large one」經典改寫 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
有人問我，怎麼在股市裡賺到一筆小錢。
我說很簡單：先準備一筆大錢。
進場兩年，我成功了，我的大錢真的變成了小錢。
唯一的問題是，這個方法太有效，再玩下去，小錢都要變成零用錢了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個本金越玩越小、還在自嘲『至少有經驗』的同好
variations: 我在股市裡學會了縮小的魔法，目標物是我的本金。
別人進股市是想以小搏大，我是成功地以大搏小。

en.content:
Someone asked me how to make a small fortune in the market.
I said it's easy: start with a large one.
Two years in, I succeeded — my large fortune really did become a small one.
The only problem is the method works too well. Keep going and the small fortune turns into spare change.
en.editorNote: For everyone whose account keeps shrinking while they joke that 'at least I got the experience'
variations: I learned a shrinking spell in the market. The target was my principal.
Other people come in to turn small money into big. I successfully turned big money into small.

items.sourceNote: 2026/6/12 台股『高開留人』攻防 / 開高走低套人 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
早盤跳空高開，紅通通的，新聞說氣勢如虹。
我怕錯過，開盤就追了進去。
結果它高開只是為了留人，留的那個人就是我。
收盤一根長黑，我才懂，原來『高開留人』的『人』，從頭到尾講的都是我這種人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在又被開高走低套在開盤價的傍晚，貼在群組互相取暖
variations: 跳空高開的紅，是專門用來引我上鉤的餌。
我追的不是強勢，是別人精心鋪好的逃命通道。

en.content:
The market gapped up at the open, all red, headlines screaming unstoppable momentum.
Terrified of missing out, I chased in right at the bell.
Turns out it only gapped up to keep someone holding the bag — and that someone was me.
A long black candle at the close, and I finally understood: the 'someone' in 'gap up to trap someone' was always a guy exactly like me.
en.editorNote: For the evening you got bagged at the opening price by another gap-up-fade — share in the chat for mutual comfort
variations: The red of a gap-up open is just bait laid out specifically to hook me.
I wasn't chasing strength. I was chasing the escape route someone else carefully built.

items.sourceNote: 2026『先修正再出發』達人觀點 / 套牢者拿來自我安慰 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
達人說：好股票要先修正再出發。
我深套的這檔，已經修正了八個月。
我每天看著它，告訴自己它正在蓄力，馬上就要出發。
後來我終於懂了，原來它不是在修正，它是真的不想出發，是我一個人在月台上等了一班永遠不會來的車。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個用『它在修正、之後會噴』催眠自己的長期套牢者
variations: 我這檔不是在修正，是在原地修身養性，順便修掉我的本金。
達人說的『修正再出發』我信了一半——修正是真的，出發是我自己加的。

en.content:
The guru says: good stocks correct first, then take off.
The one I'm deeply underwater on has been 'correcting' for eight months.
Every day I look at it and tell myself it's coiling up, about to launch any minute.
Eventually I got it: it isn't correcting. It genuinely has no intention of launching. I'm just one guy on the platform waiting for a train that's never coming.
en.editorNote: For the long-term bagholder hypnotizing themselves with 'it's correcting, it'll rip later'
variations: Mine isn't correcting. It's standing perfectly still, meditating, quietly correcting away my principal too.
I believed half of the guru's 'correct then launch' — the correcting part is real; the launching part I added myself.

items.sourceNote: 2026/6/10 台股大跌1478點史上第六大跌點 / 散戶心情雲霄飛車 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說，這幾天台股像在坐雲霄飛車。
我想了想，不對。
雲霄飛車至少還會回到原點，而且坐完你會笑。
我這台只往下開，沒有安全帶，下車的時候本金留在車上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個在史上第六大跌點那天，心臟跟著大盤一起自由落體的同好
variations: 別人說大盤像雲霄飛車，我覺得比較像高空彈跳，只是那條繩子是我自己接的。
雲霄飛車是花錢買刺激，我的帳戶是花本金買刺激，刺激度高很多。

en.content:
The news says the market's been a rollercoaster these past few days.
I thought about it. That's not right.
A rollercoaster at least returns to where it started, and you laugh when you get off.
Mine only goes down, there's no harness, and when I step off my principal stays on the ride.
en.editorNote: For everyone whose heart free-fell along with the index on the sixth-largest single-day drop in history
variations: People call the market a rollercoaster. Mine feels more like a bungee jump — except I tied the cord myself.
A rollercoaster is paying for thrills. My account is paying with principal for thrills, and the thrill level is much higher.

items.sourceNote: 暴跌隔天反彈 / 散戶『滿血復活』後又被套 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
前一天暴跌，我帳戶剩半條命。
隔天反彈，新聞說股民『滿血復活』，我跟著加碼想趁勢回本。
結果反彈只彈了半天，下午又倒下去。
所謂滿血復活，原來是讓你血條補滿，方便下一根長黑一次扣到底。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個在反彈日急著回本、結果又被洗一次的同好，貼在群組互相取暖
variations: 反彈不是來救我的，是來確認我還站著，好把我再打趴一次。
我以為是滿血復活，其實是回光返照。

en.content:
The day before, the crash left my account on half health.
The next day it bounced, headlines said traders were 'back to full HP,' so I added more to ride the recovery.
The bounce lasted half a session, then collapsed again in the afternoon.
Turns out 'back to full health' just means refilling your bar so the next long red candle can drain it all in one hit.
en.editorNote: For everyone who rushed to break even on the bounce day and got washed out again — share in the chat for mutual comfort
variations: The bounce wasn't here to save me. It was here to confirm I was still standing, so it could knock me down once more.
I thought it was a full revival. It was just the last flicker before the lights went out.

items.sourceNote: 暴跌後專家喊『黃金買點』register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
大跌之後，專家在電視上說：這是難得的黃金買點。
我信了，進場抄底。
結果黃金買點下面，還有一個鑽石買點，鑽石下面還有一個鉑金買點。
我抄了一路的底，最後才發現，這座礦坑沒有底，只有越挖越深的我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在每次聽到『黃金買點』就手癢、結果一路接刀的時候提醒自己
variations: 專家的黃金買點是一種傳說，越往下追，等級越稀有。
別人喊黃金買點是在喊給別人接，我每次都熱心地舉手說我來。

en.content:
After the big drop, the expert on TV said: this is a rare golden buying opportunity.
I believed him and bought the dip.
Turns out below the golden buy point there's a diamond buy point, and below that a platinum one.
I bought every bottom along the way, only to discover this mine has no floor — just me, digging deeper.
en.editorNote: For when 'golden buying opportunity' makes your trigger finger itch and you end up catching the whole falling knife
variations: The expert's golden buy point is a legend — the further it falls, the rarer the tier gets.
When people shout 'golden buy point,' it's a call for someone else to catch the bag. I enthusiastically raise my hand every time.

items.sourceNote: PTT『預判2026/6/27開崩』末日預言 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
股板上每隔一陣子就有人發文，預言某月某日大盤一定會崩。
這次預言的是六月二十七號。
我看完默默地把那檔深套的股票留著，想說反正要崩，乾脆放給它崩，崩完一起解脫。
結果預言之神最厲害的地方在於：它崩它的，我套我的，兩件事其實也沒什麼關係。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個一邊轉發末日預言、一邊還是死抱不放的同好
variations: 末日預言每年都有，準的那次大家都記得，不準的那幾百次大家都很大方地忘記。
我等的不是崩盤，是一個可以名正言順躺平的理由。

en.content:
Every so often someone on the trading forum posts a prophecy: the market will definitely crash on a specific date.
This time the chosen date was June 27th.
I read it, quietly kept my deeply underwater stock, and figured if it's going to crash anyway, I'll just let it crash and we'll find peace together.
Turns out the genius of prophecy gods is this: the market crashes on its own, I stay trapped on my own, and the two events have nothing to do with each other.
en.editorNote: For everyone who reposts doomsday prophecies while still refusing to let go of their bags
variations: There's a doomsday prophecy every year. Everyone remembers the one that hit; everyone graciously forgets the few hundred that missed.
What I'm waiting for isn't a crash. It's a legitimate excuse to finally lie flat.

items.sourceNote: 暴跌日外資三大法人獲利了結賣超 / 散戶『了結獲利』反諷 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞說，這波外資跟法人都在『獲利了結』。
我也想了結，問題是我沒有獲利可以了結。
我能了結的，只有我的信心、我的睡眠、跟我對人生的期待。
後來我懂了，所謂散戶，就是別人獲利了結，我了結獲利的反義詞——直接了結。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在看到法人爽爽賣超落袋、自己只能含淚抱住的時候自嘲
variations: 法人是獲利了結，我是含淚了結，差一個字，差一個人生。
別人賣在高點叫停利，我賣在低點叫停損，原來同一個動作，可以有兩種完全不同的結局。

en.content:
The news says foreign and institutional investors are all 'taking profits.'
I'd love to take some too — problem is, I have no profit to take.
All I have left to take is my confidence, my sleep, and my expectations for life.
Then I understood what being retail means: others take profit, while I take losses — same verb, opposite ending.
en.editorNote: For when you watch institutions cheerfully sell into strength and pocket gains while you can only hold on, in tears
variations: Institutions take profits, I take losses — one word apart, an entire life apart.
When others sell at the top it's called a profit stop; when I sell at the bottom it's called a stop loss. Same action, two completely different endings.

items.sourceNote: 6/18 台股 46,465 創高 vs 6/10 暴跌3.31% 一週洗三溫暖 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這一週的大盤，先暴跌再暴漲，一週洗三溫暖洗到我皮都皺了。
上禮拜跌的時候我以為是世界末日，這禮拜漲的時候我以為錯過天堂。
結論：跌的時候我嚇到不敢買，漲的時候我追到剛好套。
原來三溫暖最熱的那池，永遠是留給我跳進去的那一刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在一週內看著大盤上沖下洗、自己每次都站錯邊的週五傍晚發
variations: 大盤洗三溫暖，我洗到的是冷水，而且是我自己跳進去的。
暴跌我不敢進場，暴漲我忍不住追高，我的進出場時機跟大盤完美互斥。

en.content:
This week the index crashed, then ripped — a full sauna cycle that left me wrinkled like a prune.
Last week when it dropped, I thought it was the end of the world. This week when it pumped, I thought I'd missed heaven.
Conclusion: when it falls I'm too scared to buy, when it rises I chase right into the bag.
Turns out the hottest pool in the sauna is always reserved for the exact moment I jump in.
en.editorNote: For the Friday evening after a week of whipsaw where you stood on the wrong side every single time
variations: The market ran a full sauna cycle. I ended up in the cold plunge — one I jumped into myself.
Too scared to buy the crash, too greedy to skip the rip. My timing is the perfect inverse of the index.

items.sourceNote: 散戶多空比反指標 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
法人都說散戶是反指標，看散戶在幹嘛，反著做就對了。
我聽了很不服氣，於是這次我反著自己做：我覺得會漲的，我就不買。
結果它真的漲了，而我因為相信自己是反指標，沒上車。
原來身為反指標最痛苦的地方是——連反過來用都會錯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被笑是反指標、想反著操作卻又被打臉的同好
variations: 我是這麼可靠的反指標，可靠到法人應該付我一份顧問費。
我終於找到穩賺的方法了：把我的對帳單反過來看。

en.content:
The pros all say retail is a contrarian indicator — watch what retail does and do the opposite.
I took offense, so this time I faded myself: whatever I thought would go up, I refused to buy.
It went up. And because I believed I was the inverse signal, I missed the whole move.
Turns out the worst part of being a reliable contrarian indicator is that even running yourself in reverse still loses.
en.editorNote: For everyone mocked as a reverse indicator who tried to flip their own trades and got burned anyway
variations: I'm such a dependable contrarian signal that the institutions should be paying me a consulting fee.
I finally found a foolproof strategy: read my account statement upside down.

items.sourceNote: 「用經典圖預判開崩」末日預言文 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
股板上每隔一陣子就會有人發文：用經典歷史圖預判，某月某日大盤即將開崩。
我看完緊張到全部出清。
結果那天到了，大盤不但沒崩，還收了一根長紅。
我終於懂了：唯一會準時開崩的，是我看完末日文之後的帳戶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在看到群組瘋傳末日預言文、手指又開始發癢想清倉時提醒自己
variations: 末日預言每年都有好幾個版本，唯一真實降臨的災難是我自己嚇到砍在最低點。
我不是被大盤割的，我是被『大盤要崩了』這五個字割的。

en.content:
Every so often someone on the trading forum posts: based on a classic historical chart, the market will crash on this exact date.
I got so nervous I dumped everything.
The date arrived. The market didn't crash — it closed on a fat green candle.
Now I get it: the only thing that crashes on schedule is my account, right after I finish reading the doom post.
en.editorNote: For when a doom-prophecy post is circulating the group chat and your finger starts itching to sell everything
variations: There's a new version of the apocalypse every year. The only disaster that actually shows up is me panic-selling the bottom.
The market didn't cut me. The phrase 'the market is about to crash' cut me.

items.sourceNote: 「long-term relationship with my losses / buy regret sell hope」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我談過最長的一段感情，是跟我的虧損。
它從不離開我，跌再深都對我不離不棄。
別人的戀愛是買進希望、賣出快樂；我的投資是買進後悔、賣出絕望。
我跟我的套牢，已經到了論及婚嫁的地步，畢竟我們都捨不得分開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個帳上虧損抱到都有感情了的長期套牢戶
variations: 我跟我的庫存是真愛，因為只有它跌成這樣我還不離不棄。
別人買股票是為了賺錢，我買股票是為了培養一段刻骨銘心的關係。

en.content:
The longest relationship I've ever had is the one with my losses.
They never leave me — no matter how deep it drops, they stand by me, loyal to the end.
Other people's love story is buying hope and selling joy. My investing is buying regret and selling despair.
Me and my underwater position are basically discussing marriage at this point — neither of us can bear to part.
en.editorNote: For the long-term bagholder who has been underwater so long they've developed feelings for it
variations: Me and my position have true love, because it's the only thing that's crashed this hard and still has me staying.
Other people buy stocks to make money. I buy stocks to cultivate a deep, unforgettable relationship.

items.sourceNote: 6/10 暴跌3.31% 跌停17家 / 信仰崩盤 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
大跌那天，我跟自己說：沒事，我是長期投資，這種短期波動不影響我的信仰。
講完我手抖著按了重新整理，又重新整理，連續整理了二十分鐘。
原來我的信仰很堅定，堅定到每三秒就要確認一次它還在不在。
所謂長期投資者，就是用長期的時間，承受短期的心臟病。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在重挫日盯著綠油油的庫存、嘴上說沒事身體很誠實的時候自嘲
variations: 我對價值投資的信仰很堅定，跟我重新整理的頻率成正比。
別人的信仰寫在心裡，我的信仰寫在每三秒一次的下拉刷新上。

en.content:
On the big down day, I told myself: it's fine, I'm a long-term investor, short-term swings don't shake my conviction.
Then my trembling hand hit refresh, and refresh again, for twenty straight minutes.
Turns out my conviction is rock solid — so solid I need to confirm it's still there every three seconds.
A long-term investor is just someone enduring a short-term heart condition over a long period of time.
en.editorNote: For the crash day when your mouth says 'I'm fine' but your body keeps refreshing the deep-green portfolio
variations: My faith in value investing is unshakable — directly proportional to how often I hit refresh.
Other people keep their conviction in their heart. I keep mine in a pull-to-refresh every three seconds.

items.sourceNote: PTT 股板經典幹話「庫存綠到有牛在上面吃草」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我最近股票還好嗎。
我說還行，就是庫存綠得有點過頭。
綠到什麼程度？綠到我懷疑點開的不是券商 APP，是空拍的高山牧場。
再這樣下去，我打算在帳戶裡放幾頭牛，至少讓那片綠地有點生產力。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在庫存一片綠油油時丟進投資群組，給同樣在放牧的同好
variations: 我的對帳單已經綠到可以申請當生態保護區了。
別人看盤看紅綠，我看盤已經升級到看草原了。

en.content:
A friend asked how my stocks were doing lately.
I said fine, the portfolio's just a touch too green.
How green? Green enough that I'm not sure I opened the brokerage app — might be a drone shot of an alpine pasture.
If this keeps up, I'm putting a few cows in the account, just to make all that green a little more productive.
en.editorNote: Drop this in the group chat on a sea-of-green day, for the fellow ranchers out there
variations: My statement is green enough to qualify as a protected nature reserve.
Other people read the candles as red or green. My screen has graduated to grassland.

items.sourceNote: 「If experts were so expert they'd buy stock not sell advice」反諷 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
投顧老師在直播裡信心滿滿：「跟著我，財富自由就在前方。」
我心想，如果你真的這麼神，幹嘛還在這裡賣課，自己默默買進場就好了啊。
後來我懂了，他確實有財富自由——靠的是我們繳的學費，不是他報的明牌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個繳了會員費才發現老師賺的是學費不是行情的同好
variations: 真正會賺錢的人都在悶聲發財，會上電視喊單的，賺的是你的注意力。
如果明牌真的會漲，他自己梭哈就好，何必特地告訴你。

en.content:
The guru on the livestream is brimming with confidence: 'Follow me and financial freedom is right around the corner.'
I thought, if you're really that good, why are you here selling a course instead of quietly loading up yourself?
Then it hit me — he does have financial freedom. It just comes from our tuition, not his stock picks.
en.editorNote: For everyone who paid the membership fee only to learn the guru profits off tuition, not the market
variations: The people who actually make money do it in silence. The ones shouting buy calls on TV are profiting off your attention.
If the hot tip were really going to moon, he'd go all in himself instead of bothering to tell you.

items.sourceNote: 「韭菜指標：賺一點就跑、賠了死抱」人性弱點 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我的操作有一套非常穩定的邏輯。
賺錢的股票，我怕它回吐，小賺一點就趕快跑。
賠錢的股票，我相信它會回來，跌再深都死命抱著。
結果就是：會漲的全部留不住，會跌的全部丟不掉，完美。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個停利快、停損慢，把賺的賣光、把賠的留滿的同好
variations: 我手上的股票有一個共同點：它們都是我捨不得賣的賠錢貨。
賺錢的留不住，賠錢的捨不得，這就是我穩定賠錢的秘訣。

en.content:
My trading follows one rock-solid principle.
Winning stocks? Scared they'll give it back, so I bail the second I'm up a little.
Losing stocks? Convinced they'll come back, so I cling on no matter how far they drop.
Net result: I can't keep the ones that rise, can't get rid of the ones that fall. Flawless.
en.editorNote: For everyone quick to take profits and slow to take losses — selling the winners, hoarding the losers
variations: Everything in my account has one thing in common: it's a loser I couldn't bear to sell.
Can't hold the winners, can't let go of the losers — that's the secret to my consistent losses.

items.sourceNote: 「holding long-term = refuse to panic publicly」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
每次別人問我那檔慘賠的股票要不要賣，我都一臉淡定地說：我是長期投資者，不在乎短期波動。
講得很有大師風範。
其實『長期投資者』的真實意思是：我已經賠到不敢在公開場合崩潰，只能私下偷哭。
表面的雲淡風輕，是用裡面的兵荒馬亂換來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個嘴上說淡定、回到房間就打開對帳單嘆氣的長期持有者
variations: 所謂的『佛系存股』，就是賠到只剩下信佛這個選項。
我不是不慌，我只是把慌留給沒有人看到的時候。

en.content:
Every time someone asks if I'm going to sell that deeply underwater stock, I say, perfectly calm: I'm a long-term investor, short-term noise doesn't faze me.
Very zen-master energy.
The real meaning of 'long-term investor' is: I'm down too much to melt down in public, so I cry quietly in private.
That serene surface is paid for by total chaos underneath.
en.editorNote: For the holder who plays it cool out loud, then opens the statement and sighs the moment they're alone
variations: 'Zen-style dividend investing' is what happens when you're down so far that faith is the only option left.
It's not that I'm not panicking. I just save the panic for when nobody's watching.

items.sourceNote: 「buy high cry later / portfolio has trust issues」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我的投資組合最近有點信任危機。
它不相信我會解套，我也不相信它會回本，我們兩個就這樣互相懷疑，僵在那裡。
我的投資哲學也很一致：買在最高，哭在後頭。
買的時候充滿希望，賣的時候只剩經驗——很貴的那種經驗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在追高被套、看著對帳單跟它大眼瞪小眼的時候自嘲
variations: 我跟我的庫存之間，已經沒有信任，只剩債務關係。
買高哭後面，這八個字總結了我整段投資生涯。

en.content:
My portfolio has been having some trust issues lately.
It doesn't believe I'll ever break even, I don't believe it'll ever recover, so we just sit here side-eyeing each other.
My investing philosophy is equally consistent: buy high, cry later.
Full of hope when I buy, left with nothing but experience when I sell — the very expensive kind.
en.editorNote: For staring down your statement after chasing the top and getting trapped
variations: There's no trust left between me and my position — just a debt relationship.
Buy high, cry later. Four words that sum up my entire investing career.

items.sourceNote: 6月台指期破紀錄崩跌 / 少年股神畢業文 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
前陣子我還是同事口中的『少年股神』，每天進辦公室都有人問我下一檔買什麼。
台指期那根破紀錄的長黑，一夜之間把我的神格降回凡人，還順便扣到欠錢。
現在沒人問我明牌了，他們改問我：「你還好嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個曾經是群組明燈、最近默默潛水的同學
variations: 從股神到股民只要一根黑K，從股民到股奴只要再一根。
我的封號更新得很快：少年股神，現在叫少年股債。

en.content:
