Not long ago my coworkers called me the 'boy genius trader' — every morning someone asked what I was buying next.
One record-breaking red candle on the futures overnight demoted me from god back to mortal, then kept going until I owed money.
Nobody asks me for tips anymore. Now they just ask, 'Hey, are you okay?'
en.editorNote: For everyone who used to be the group chat's guru and has quietly gone silent lately
variations: From trading god to trader takes one red candle. From trader to debtor takes one more.
My title updates fast: yesterday boy genius, today boy in debt.

items.sourceNote: PTT「別人恐懼我貪婪，別人小虧我破產」反諷名言 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
巴菲特說：別人恐懼我貪婪，別人貪婪我恐懼。
我認真照做，把這句話貼在螢幕上每天看。
執行成果：別人恐懼的時候，我也恐懼；別人貪婪的時候，我才終於貪婪。
所以我永遠慢半拍，恐懼接在最低點，貪婪接在最高點，全程精準錯開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個把投資名言背得滾瓜爛熟、實際操作卻完全相反的同好
variations: 名言我都會背，只是市場不照名言走，我也不照名言做。
別人恐懼我貪婪這句話很對，只可惜我總是先恐懼完，才想起要貪婪。

en.content:
Buffett said: be greedy when others are fearful, fearful when others are greedy.
I took it seriously and taped it to my monitor.
Execution results: when others were fearful, I was fearful too; only once everyone got greedy did I finally feel brave.
So I'm always one beat late — catching fear at the bottom, greed at the top, perfectly out of sync the whole way.
en.editorNote: For everyone who has the investing quotes memorized and does the exact opposite in practice
variations: I can recite every famous quote. The market just doesn't follow them, and neither do I.
'Be greedy when others are fearful' is great advice. Shame I always finish being fearful right before I remember to be greedy.

items.sourceNote: PTT「庫存綠到有牛在上面吃草」幹話語錄 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我最近持股顏色怎麼樣。
我說綠的，很環保的那種綠。
綠到我懷疑帳戶裡長出了草原，再過幾天可能會有牛跑進來吃草。
我這不是在投資，我是在經營一座生態保護區。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在收盤後對著一片綠油油的對帳單苦中作樂時發
variations: 我的庫存綠到可以申請成為國家公園。
別人的紅燈是賺錢，我的綠燈是養草，各有各的收成。

en.content:
A friend asked what color my holdings are these days.
I said green — the eco-friendly kind.
So green I suspect a meadow has sprouted in my account, and in a few days a cow might wander in to graze.
I'm not running a portfolio. I'm running a nature reserve.
en.editorNote: For making the best of it while staring at a wall of green after the close
variations: My holdings are green enough to apply for national park status.
Other people's red means profit. My green means I'm growing grass. Different harvests, I suppose.

items.sourceNote: 「buy the dip → bottomless pit」自嘲 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
理財文章教我：別怕震盪，逢低買進就對了。
於是震盪來了，我逢低買進。
再來一個震盪，我又逢低買進。
買到後來我才發現，我以為的那個低，其實沒有底——它不是一個價位，是一個無底洞，而我把全部家當都丟進去填它。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在每次想加碼攤平、覺得『這次真的是最低了』的那一秒
variations: 我不是在逢低買進，我是在填一個永遠填不滿的洞。
震盪是市場給有勇氣的人的禮物，我收了一堆禮物，現在禮物比本金多。

en.content:
The finance blog taught me: don't fear volatility, just buy the dip.
So the volatility came, and I bought the dip.
Another shake, and I bought the dip again.
Eventually I realized the dip I kept buying had no floor — it wasn't a price, it was a bottomless pit, and I'd thrown my entire net worth in trying to fill it.
en.editorNote: Read this the second you decide to average down because 'this really has to be the bottom'
variations: I'm not buying the dip. I'm filling a hole that can never be filled.
Volatility is the market's gift to the brave. I collected a pile of gifts. Now I have more gifts than capital.

items.sourceNote: 槓桿 / 高槓桿融資斷頭 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我開了融資，因為我相信這檔一定漲，槓桿開下去報酬翻倍。
結果它沒漲，跌了。
槓桿很公平，它把報酬翻倍，也把虧損翻倍，最後連我的本金一起翻走了。
我這才懂，槓桿不是放大鏡，是離心機——把我跟我的錢，甩到兩個不同的方向。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給每個被『開槓桿才賺得快』推坑、結果先被斷頭的勇者
variations: 槓桿讓我用兩倍的速度，抵達原本要好幾年才會破產的地方。
我以為槓桿是加速器，它確實是，只是加速的方向是往下。

en.content:
I took out margin because I was sure this one would rip — lever up and double the returns.
It didn't rip. It dropped.
Margin is fair: it doubled the gains, doubled the losses, and in the end doubled my principal right out the door.
That's when I understood leverage isn't a magnifying glass, it's a centrifuge — it flung me and my money in two different directions.
en.editorNote: For the brave soul talked into 'you only get rich fast with leverage' who got margin-called first
variations: Leverage let me reach, at double speed, a bankruptcy that would otherwise have taken years.
I thought leverage was an accelerator. It was — the direction it accelerated me was straight down.
